data.title: Goldman Sachs to team up with North Carolina Community College System to provide free small business education program to NC entrepreneurs.
data.description: In Wilmington, North Carolina, on September 24, 2025, the renowned Goldman Sachs 10,000 Small Businesses initiative has been aiding entrepreneurs for a decade and a half. This program provides a no-cost, hands-on business training program along with opportunities for funding and tailored support services to assist entrepreneurs in expanding their businesses.

data.title: Author Roger Croft, former MI6 veteran, explores Mideast turmoil in new book. Served as 2nd Lieutenant in RASC.
data.description: Roger Croft, a journalist and author based in London, has recently published a new book showcasing his extensive experience in global reporting. Following his service as a 2nd Lieutenant in the Royal Army Service Corps with the British Army of the Rhine, Croft transitioned to journalism. The book captures his adventures and insights accumulated over the years. The author's work is a culmination of his valuable experience and expertise in the field, offering readers a unique perspective.

data.title: Aster, the latest hyperliquid rival, surged 2,000% in just one week, becoming a top pick for crypto traders. How far will it rise?
data.description: Aster, a competing cryptocurrency to Hyperliquid, has quickly gained popularity among crypto enthusiasts, experiencing significant growth since its launch just seven days ago. With its increasing momentum, many are wondering if Aster can sustain its upward trajectory in the coming days.

data.title: CrossCountry Mortgage raises conforming loan limits to $819,000 from $806,500, ahead of projected 2026 changes.
data.description: Cleveland-based CrossCountry Mortgage (CCM), the leading retail mortgage lender in the country, has announced a significant update by raising its agency conforming loan limits to $819,000, a notable increase from the previous amount of $806,500. This strategic decision was made well in advance of the Federal Housing Finance Agency's upcoming adjustments.

data.title: Woodruff Sawyer's 13th D&O Looking Ahead Guide emphasizes new risks for boards, benefits buyers.
data.description: Boards are encountering increased risks in the market, but current conditions are advantageous for buyers. On September 24, 2025, Woodruff Sawyer, a comprehensive insurance brokerage and consulting company, published its 13th annual D&O Looking Ahead Guide. This guide provides boards and executives with valuable insights into the future landscape.

data.title: Ariana Grande launches two Wicked-inspired fragrances in partnership with Luxe Brands and Universal Pictures ahead of Wicked: For Good release.
data.description: On September 23, 2025, in New York, Ariana Grande, a renowned global artist with Academy Award and Grammy nominations, launched two exclusive fragrances in anticipation of the upcoming finale of the popular cultural phenomenon, Wicked. The limited-edition scents, Glinda Bubbly Pink and Elphaba Enchanted, were revealed to the public.

data.title: Shares of TZUP surge as Thumzup announces buyback plans; investors bullish on Dogecoin & Bitcoin treasury firm.
data.description: Investors have shown positive approval of Thumzup, leading to a rise in TZUP shares. The Dogecoin and Bitcoin treasury company intends to initiate a stock buyback, indicating promising growth for the firm.

data.title: Dow Jones slides post new home sales data with AI stock Micron drop on earnings. Oracle, OpenAI have plan.
data.description: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones index experienced a decline following the release of new home sales data. Micron, an AI stock, saw a drop in value after reporting its earnings. This decline in the stock market occurred as inflation data was anticipated. Additionally, Oracle and OpenAI unveiled a plan, causing fluctuations in the market. The Dow slid while these events unfolded, emphasizing the impact of economic indicators and corporate announcements on stock performance.

data.title: Bitcoin and XRP lead crypto rebound, with Bitcoin stabilizing above $113,000. 108,199 traders liquidated in 24 hours. XRP at $2.95, and Ethereum at $4,170.99.
data.description: The cryptocurrency market is showing an upward trend today, with Bitcoin holding steady above $113,000. Current prices for top cryptocurrencies include Bitcoin at $113,651.45, Ethereum at $4,170.99, Solana at $214.09, XRP at $2.95, Dogecoin at $0.2446, and Shiba Inu at $0.00001225. According to Coinglass data, 108,199 traders were liquidated, resulting in a total loss of $259.27 million in the last 24 hours. Nate Geraci mentioned the possibility of final amendments being filed by the end of the week for spot crypto ETFs like XRP and Solana. Some of the highest gainers in the past day were Aster, Flare, and Immutable. Noteworthy news includes XRP's 4% increase, the expectation of $3 as a crucial level for ETF approvals, and an advisor to the Trump family making a bold 10x long bet on Avalanche. For more details, the complete story can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Matthews Marking Systems introduces Axian Inkjet technology, revolutionizing product marking for CPG and FMCG industries.
data.description: Today in Pittsburgh, on September 24, 2025, Matthews International Corporation (NASDAQ GSM: MATW) revealed that its division, Matthews Marking Systems, is introducing the Axian Inkjet (XIJ) printing technology. This groundbreaking advancement revolutionizes fast and high-quality product marking for industries such as consumer-packaged goods (CPG) and fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG). This innovation promises to set a new standard in speed and resolution for product marking in these sectors.
